channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge sentences Trump to unconditional discharge, no punishment in hush money conviction
item.description: President-elect Trump was spared any punishment for his hush money criminal conviction Friday when a New York judge sentenced him to an unconditional discharge, eschewing jail time while securing his status as the first person convicted of a felony to assume the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sentences Trump in hush money case but declines to impose any punishment
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment, an outcome that cements his conviction but frees him to return to the White House unencumbered by the threat of a jail term or a fine.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, ABC 4

item.title: Biden lets 800,000 Venezuelans and Salvadorans stay 18 months as Trump readies immigration crackdown
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security says about 600,000 Venezuelans and more than 200,000 El Salvadorans already living in the United States can legally remain another 18 months.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Civilian drone hits, damages firefighting aircraft over Palisades Fire
item.description: A firefighting aircraft that was operating over the Palisades Fire is grounded and out of service after it was struck and damaged by a civilian’s drone on Thursday afternoon, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge sentences Trump in hush money case but declines to impose any punishment
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment, an outcome that cements his conviction but frees him to return to the White House unencumbered by the threat of a jail term or a fine.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Fox 5

item.title: Arson suspect arrested in Los Angeles neighborhood near Kenneth Fire
item.description: An arson suspect was arrested Thursday night near the Kenneth Fire area as fire crews continue battling flames burning near homes in Calabasas and West Hills.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, Fox 5

item.title: Illegal immigrant accused in attempted teen kidnapping deported 5 times: officials
item.description: A Mexican illegal immigrant deported from the United States five times has been arrested after allegedly attempting to kidnap a teenage Alabama girl on New Year's Eve.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LIVE: Fires devastating LA grow more slowly as winds die down
item.description: The two biggest fires devastating the Los Angeles area grew just slightly Friday as firefighters beat back the blazes that have killed at least 10 people, obliterated neighborhoods and left the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: The Supreme Court is considering a possible TikTok ban. Here's what to know about the case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The law that could ban TikTok is coming before the Supreme Court on Friday, with the justices largely holding the app's fate in their hands. The popular social media platform says the law violates the First Amendment and should be struck down.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jamie Lee Curtis donates $1M to LA wildfire relief efforts
item.description: Jamie Lee Curtis says her family is donating $1 million to relief efforts amid the wildfires in California that have devastated the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 10 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sharply divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s final bid to put his New York hush-money case on hold, clearing the way for him to be sentenced for felony crimes days before he returns to the presidency. The court’s 5-4 order allows Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A powerful winter storm's heavy snow and sleet cancels flights and closes schools across US South
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm brought heavy snow and icy conditions to parts of the U.S. South on Thursday, forcing officials to close schools, cancel flights and warn residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as it lumbered eastward through Oklahoma and Texas. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan woman gets jail after boy brought pink gun to school
item.description: A woman who illegally possessed a pink handgun that a child took to a Grand Rapids elementary school in a backpack has been sentenced to a year in jail.
item.dc:creator: John Hogan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: College athlete dies from tragic accident on ski resort’s most difficult trail
item.description: Alex Kemp, a 19-year-old Williams College student from New Jersey, died on Tuesday after skiing at Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort in Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PowerSchool hack exposes Indiana students' data
item.description: BROWNSBURG, Ind. -- Multiple Indianapolis area school corporations confirmed an unauthorized user accessed its student information system as part of a national data breach.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter memorial: Suspect accused in Capitol Hill security breach during Trump visit identified
item.description: Capitol police have identified the 35-year-old man accused of trying to light his spray-painted car on fire near where former President Carter was lying in state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs fans' deaths: Why former homicide detective believes criminal charges still possible
item.description: Clayton McGeeney, Ricky Johnson and David Harrington were all found dead in Jordan Willis' Kansas City backyard on Jan 9, 2024 – a year later, their families still have no answers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Celebrity homes among those lost in devastating Los Angeles fires
item.description: Wildfires that are burning in and around Los Angeles have burned the homes of several celebrities, including Billy Crystal, Mandy Moore and Paris Hilton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Stowaways on planes and inside landing gear raise worries about aviation security
item.description: What is going on with aviation security? People have been found dead hiding in the wheel wells of planes twice in the past month. Two stowaways were arrested on different flights in November and December. Then a passenger opened an emergency door while a plane was taxiing in Boston Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's life intersected with slavery's legacy. His record on Civil Rights is complicated
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Young Jimmy Carter and his friends were walking across a pasture after a day’s farm labor during the Great Depression. As they came to a gate, his companions stood aside and let Carter enter first. This was no act of kindness or intuitive deference to a future U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DeSantis, longtime Newsom rival, offers help to California amid wildfires
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) offered assistance to California on Wednesday as the state grapples with severe wildfires in the greater Los Angeles area. “Our prayers are with everyone affected by the horrific fires in Southern California,” DeSantis said in a post on social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Distracted semi-truck driver slams into another semi on side of Oklahoma highway
item.description: The Oklahoma Highway Patrol (OHP) is reminding drivers to give their full attention to the road after a dash cam caught one semi-truck crash into another on the side of the highway.
item.dc:creator: Terré Gables/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris's California neighborhood ordered to evacuate as wildfires blaze through area
item.description: Vice President Harris's neighborhood in Los Angeles was ordered to evacuate Tuesday night as wildfires blazed through the area, her spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about wildfires raining embers onto the Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, with fast-moving flames burning through homes and businesses as residents flee smoke-filled canyons and picturesque neighborhoods that are home to many celebrities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5
